title: polypodium lucidulum
intro: Polypodium lucidulum, also known as False Fox Squirrel's Foot Fern, is a species of fern native to the southeastern United States. It has been used in traditional medicine for its medicinal properties, particularly in treating respiratory issues such as bronchitis and asthma, due to its expectorant and anti-inflammatory properties. The health benefits of Polypodium lucidulum include reducing inflammation, relieving coughs, and alleviating symptoms of allergies. Polypodium lucidulum is typically prepared as a tea or tincture for its medicinal uses, although specific herbal preparations may vary depending on the region and intended application.
